003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.client;
018
019import java.io.*;
020import java.net.*;
021import java.nio.charset.*;
022import java.time.*;
023import java.util.*;
024import java.util.function.*;
025
026import org.apache.juneau.http.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.http.entity.*;
028import org.apache.juneau.http.header.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.http.part.*;
030
031/**
032 * Fluent builder for a single HTTP request.
033 *
034 * <p>
035 * Obtain instances from the {@link RestClient} factory methods (e.g. {@link RestClient#get(String)}).
036 *
037 * <p>
038 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
039 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
040 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
041 * (and possibly earlier).
042 *
043 * @since 9.2.1
044 */
045@SuppressWarnings({
046   "java:S1192", // Duplicate string literals are HTTP header names and REST protocol wire values; intentional
047   "resource"    // client is not owned here; run() transfers RestResponse ownership to caller
048})
049public final class RestRequest {
050
051   private final RestClient client;
052   private final String method;
053   private final String url;
054   private final List<HttpHeader> headers = new ArrayList<>();
055   private final List<HttpPart> queryData = new ArrayList<>();
056   private final List<HttpPart> formData = new ArrayList<>();
057   private final Map<String, Object> pathData = new LinkedHashMap<>();
058   private HttpBody body;
059   private TransportBody convertedBody;
060   private boolean debug;
061   private URI resolvedUri;
062   private Duration timeout;
063   // Per-request interceptors, unioned with the client-level interceptors at run() time.
064   private final List<RestCallInterceptor> requestInterceptors = new ArrayList<>();
065
066   RestRequest(RestClient client, String method, String url) {
067      this.client = client;
068      this.method = method;
069      this.url = url;
070      // Inherit client defaults
071      this.headers.addAll(client.defaultHeaders);
072      this.queryData.addAll(client.defaultQueryData);
073   }
074
075   // --------------------------------------------------
076   // Header
077   // --------------------------------------------------
078
079   /**
080    * Adds request headers.
081    *
082    * @param value The headers to add. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
083    * @return This object.
084    */
085   public RestRequest headers(HttpHeader... value) {
086      headers.addAll(Arrays.asList(value));
087      return this;
088   }
089
090   /**
091    * Adds a request header.
092    *
093    * @param name The header name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
094    * @param value The header value. May be <jk>null</jk> to omit.
095    * @return This object.
096    */
097   public RestRequest header(String name, String value) {
098      headers.add(HttpHeaderBean.of(name, value));
099      return this;
100   }
101
102   /**
103    * Adds a request header with a lazy value.
104    *
105    * @param name The header name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
106    * @param value Supplier for the value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
107    * @return This object.
108    */
109   public RestRequest header(String name, Supplier<String> value) {
110      headers.add(HttpHeaderBean.of(name, value));
111      return this;
112   }
113
114   /**
115    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if a header with the given name (case-insensitive) is already set on this request.
116    *
117    * <p>
118    * Used by the next-gen remote-proxy engine to honor the precedence of a caller-supplied
119    * {@code @Header} parameter over an annotation-declared {@code accept}/{@code contentType} attribute.
120    *
121    * @param name The header name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
122    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the header is present.
123    */
124   public boolean hasHeader(String name) {
125      return headers.stream().anyMatch(h -> name.equalsIgnoreCase(h.getName()));
126   }
127
128   // --------------------------------------------------
129   // Query
130   // --------------------------------------------------
131
132   /**
133    * Adds query parameters.
134    *
135    * @param value The parts to add. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
136    * @return This object.
137    */
138   public RestRequest queryData(HttpPart... value) {
139      queryData.addAll(Arrays.asList(value));
140      return this;
141   }
142
143   /**
144    * Adds a query parameter.
145    *
146    * @param name The parameter name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
147    * @param value The parameter value. May be <jk>null</jk> to omit.
148    * @return This object.
149    */
150   public RestRequest queryData(String name, String value) {
151      queryData.add(HttpPartBean.of(name, value));
152      return this;
153   }
154
155   /**
156    * Adds a query parameter with a lazy value.
157    *
158    * @param name The parameter name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
159    * @param value Supplier for the value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
160    * @return This object.
161    */
162   public RestRequest queryData(String name, Supplier<String> value) {
163      queryData.add(HttpPartBean.of(name, value));
164      return this;
165   }
166
167   // --------------------------------------------------
168   // Form data
169   // --------------------------------------------------
170
171   /**
172    * Adds form fields (for {@code application/x-www-form-urlencoded} bodies).
173    *
174    * @param value The parts to add. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
175    * @return This object.
176    */
177   public RestRequest formData(HttpPart... value) {
178      formData.addAll(Arrays.asList(value));
179      return this;
180   }
181
182   /**
183    * Adds a form field.
184    *
185    * @param name The field name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
186    * @param value The field value. May be <jk>null</jk> to omit.
187    * @return This object.
188    */
189   public RestRequest formData(String name, String value) {
190      formData.add(HttpPartBean.of(name, value));
191      return this;
192   }
193
194   // --------------------------------------------------
195   // Path substitution
196   // --------------------------------------------------
197
198   /**
199    * Adds path variable substitutions for URI templates (e.g. replaces {@code {id}} in the URL).
200    *
201    * @param value The path parts to apply. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
202    * @return This object.
203    */
204   public RestRequest pathData(HttpPart... value) {
205      for (var p : value)
206         pathData.put(p.getName(), p.getValue());
207      return this;
208   }
209
210   /**
211    * Adds a path variable.
212    *
213    * @param name The variable name (without braces). Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
214    * @param value The replacement value. May be <jk>null</jk>.
215    * @return This object.
216    */
217   public RestRequest pathData(String name, Object value) {
218      pathData.put(name, value);
219      return this;
220   }
221
222   // --------------------------------------------------
223   // Body
224   // --------------------------------------------------
225
226   /**
227    * Sets the request body directly from an {@link HttpBody}.
228    *
229    * @param value The body. May be <jk>null</jk>.
230    * @return This object.
231    */
232   public RestRequest body(HttpBody value) {
233      body = value;
234      convertedBody = null;
235      return this;
236   }
237
238   /**
239    * Sets the request body from an arbitrary Java object.
240    *
241    * <p>
242    * The object is first run through the client's body converter chain. The default converters handle:
243    * {@link HttpBody} (passthrough), {@link InputStream}, {@code byte[]}, and {@link java.io.File}.
244    * Custom converters can be registered on the builder.
245    *
246    * <p>
247    * If no converter matches, the object is serialized with the client's explicitly-configured default serializer
248    * ({@link RestClient#getDefaultSerializer()}) and sent as a string body using the serializer's content type.  When
249    * no default serializer is configured, an {@link IllegalStateException} is raised (there is no implicit JSON
250    * fallback) — configure one via {@link RestClient.Builder#defaultSerializer(org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer)}.
251    *
252    * @param value The body object. May be <jk>null</jk> to clear the body.
253    * @return This object.
254    * @throws IOException If a converter fails or the default serializer fails.
255    * @throws IllegalStateException If no converter matches and no default serializer is configured on the client.
256    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the default serializer produces output that is neither text nor {@code byte[]}.
257    *    Binary ({@code byte[]}) serializer output is sent as a binary body using the serializer's media type
258    *    (falling back to {@code application/octet-stream} when the media type is <jk>null</jk>).
259    */
260   public RestRequest body(Object value) throws IOException {
261      if (value == null) {
262         body = null;
263         convertedBody = null;
264         return this;
265      }
266      for (var converter : client.bodyConverters) {
267         if (converter.canConvert(value)) {
268            convertedBody = converter.convert(value);
269            body = null;
270            return this;
271         }
272      }
273      // No converter matched: stream the POJO through the client's default serializer.
274      // Mirrors classic SerializedEntity — the serializer writes straight to the transport output stream during
275      // run() rather than being pre-materialized into a String/byte[].  Repeatable (re-serializes on resend), so a
276      // future auto-retry can resend it; streaming bodies (InputStream/Reader) remain non-repeatable.
277      var s = client.getDefaultSerializer().orElseThrow(() -> new IllegalStateException(
278         "No default serializer is configured on the client.  Configure one via RestClient.Builder.defaultSerializer(...)."));
279      var mt = s.getResponseContentType();
280      body = SerializerBody.of(s, value, mt != null ? mt.toString() : "application/json");
281      convertedBody = null;
282      return this;
283   }
284
285   /**
286    * Sets the request body to a token/record-streaming cursor body.
287    *
288    * <p>
289    * Convenience for {@link #body(HttpBody) body(streamBody)} that documents streaming intent and avoids a cast.
290    * The {@link RecordStreamBody} writes directly to the transport output stream during {@link #run()}, so large
291    * payloads are streamed to the wire without being buffered in memory.
292    *
293    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
294    * <p class='bjava'>
295    *    <jv>client</jv>.post(<js>"/bulk-upload"</js>)
296    *       .streamBodyEntity(RecordStreamBody.<jsm>records</jsm>(<jv>w</jv> -&gt; {
297    *          <jk>for</jk> (Bean <jv>b</jv> : <jv>source</jv>())
298    *             <jv>w</jv>.write(<jv>b</jv>);
299    *       }))
300    *       .run();
301    * </p>
302    *
303    * @param value The streaming body. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
304    * @return This object.
305    */
306   public RestRequest streamBodyEntity(RecordStreamBody value) {
307      return body(value);
308   }
309
310   /**
311    * Sets the request body to a raw string (UTF-8, {@code text/plain}).
312    *
313    * <p>
314    * Unlike {@link #body(Object)}, this bypasses the converter chain and sends the string as-is.
315    *
316    * @param value The string body. May be <jk>null</jk> to clear the body.
317    * @return This object.
318    */
319   public RestRequest bodyString(String value) {
320      if (value == null) {
321         body = null;
322         convertedBody = null;
323      } else {
324         body = StringBody.of(value, "text/plain");
325         convertedBody = null;
326      }
327      return this;
328   }
329
330   // --------------------------------------------------
331   // Debug
332   // --------------------------------------------------
333
334   /**
335    * Flags this request for verbose debug logging.
336    *
337    * <p>
338    * When set, the configured {@link RestLogger} will receive an entry with {@link RestLogEntry#isDebug()} {@code true},
339    * enabling full request/response header and body logging for this call only.
340    *
341    * @return This object.
342    */
343   public RestRequest debug() {
344      debug = true;
345      return this;
346   }
347
348   // --------------------------------------------------
349   // Timeout / interceptors
350   // --------------------------------------------------
351
352   /**
353    * Sets a per-call response timeout for this request.
354    *
355    * <p>
356    * Threaded onto the {@link TransportRequest} and applied by the transport as the response/read timeout.  Connect
357    * timeouts remain a client-level setting.
358    *
359    * @param value The response timeout. May be <jk>null</jk> to use the transport default.
360    * @return This object.
361    */
362   public RestRequest timeout(Duration value) {
363      timeout = value;
364      return this;
365   }
366
367   /**
368    * Adds per-request lifecycle interceptors.
369    *
370    * <p>
371    * These are unioned with the client-level interceptors when the request runs: the client-level (builder)
372    * interceptors fire first, then these in the order added.  The next-gen remote-proxy engine uses this to layer
373    * annotation-declared interface-level then method-level interceptors after the builder-configured ones.
374    *
375    * @param value The interceptors to add. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
376    * @return This object.
377    */
378   public RestRequest interceptors(RestCallInterceptor... value) {
379      requestInterceptors.addAll(Arrays.asList(value));
380      return this;
381   }
382
383   /**
384    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this request's body can be safely re-sent (retry gating).
385    *
386    * <p>
387    * A request with no body is trivially repeatable.  Otherwise the answer is delegated to the underlying
388    * body's {@link HttpBody#isRepeatable()} (or {@link TransportBody#isRepeatable()} for a pre-converted body):
389    * buffered bodies (string/byte[]/file/serialized POJO) are repeatable; one-shot streaming bodies
390    * ({@link InputStream}/{@link Reader}/piped) are not.
391    *
392    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the body is absent or repeatable.
393    */
394   public boolean isBodyRepeatable() {
395      if (convertedBody != null)
396         return convertedBody.isRepeatable();
397      if (body != null)
398         return body.isRepeatable();
399      return true;
400   }
401
402   // --------------------------------------------------
403   // Accessors (for logging / interceptors)
404   // --------------------------------------------------
405
406   /**
407    * Returns the HTTP method for this request (e.g. {@code "GET"}).
408    *
409    * @return The method. Never <jk>null</jk>.
410    */
411   public String getMethod() {
412      return method;
413   }
414
415   /**
416    * Returns the fully resolved URI for this request, or {@code null} if {@link #run()} has not been called yet.
417    *
418    * @return The URI, possibly <jk>null</jk>.
419    */
420   public URI getUri() {
421      return resolvedUri;
422   }
423
424   // --------------------------------------------------
425   // Execute
426   // --------------------------------------------------
427
428   /**
429    * Executes the request and returns the response.
430    *
431    * <p>
432    * The caller is responsible for closing the returned {@link RestResponse}.
433    *
434    * @return The response. Never <jk>null</jk>.
435    * @throws TransportException If a network-level error occurs.
436    * @throws RestCallException If the response could not be processed.
437    */
438   @SuppressWarnings({
439      "java:S1130" // RestCallException retained for public-API/source stability: interceptors declare 'throws Exception', so a RestCallException they raise is caught by the precise-rethrow multi-catch below and propagated unchanged; Sonar's flow analysis misses this path. Removing the throws would break callers that catch RestCallException (a sibling checked exception of TransportException).
440   })
441   public RestResponse run() throws TransportException, RestCallException {
442      var start = Instant.now();
443      RestResponse response = null;
444      Throwable error = null;
445      // Union the client-level (builder) interceptors with any per-request (interface- then
446      // method-level) interceptors, preserving the order builder → interface → method.
447      var effectiveInterceptors = effectiveInterceptors();
448      try {
449         for (var interceptor : effectiveInterceptors)
450            interceptor.onInit(this);
451
452         var transportRequest = buildTransportRequest();
453         var transportResponse = client.transport.execute(transportRequest);
454         response = new RestResponse(transportResponse, client);
455
456         for (var interceptor : effectiveInterceptors)
457            interceptor.onConnect(this, response);
458
459         return response;
460      } catch (TransportException | RestCallException e) {
461         error = e;
462         throw e;
463      } catch (Exception e) {
464         error = e;
465         throw new TransportException("Request failed: " + e.getMessage(), e);
466      } finally {
467         var elapsed = Duration.between(start, Instant.now());
468         RestResponse finalResponse = response;
469         for (var interceptor : effectiveInterceptors) {
470            try {
471               interceptor.onClose(this, finalResponse);
472            } catch (Exception e2) { // HTT: exception in onClose; hard to test reliably
473               // suppress interceptor close errors — best effort
474            }
475         }
476         if (client.logger != null) {
477            var entry = RestLogEntry.builder()
478               .request(this)
479               .response(finalResponse)
480               .error(error)
481               .elapsed(elapsed)
482               .debug(debug)
483               .build();
484            client.logger.log(entry);
485         }
486      }
487   }
488
489   /** Returns the client-level interceptors followed by the per-request ones (builder → interface → method order). */
490   private List<RestCallInterceptor> effectiveInterceptors() {
491      if (requestInterceptors.isEmpty())
492         return client.interceptors;
493      var l = new ArrayList<RestCallInterceptor>(client.interceptors.size() + requestInterceptors.size());
494      l.addAll(client.interceptors);
495      l.addAll(requestInterceptors);
496      return l;
497   }
498
499   @SuppressWarnings({
500      "java:S3776" // Request-assembly branching is intentional and cohesive; refactoring would reduce readability without benefit.
501   })
502   private TransportRequest buildTransportRequest() {
503      var resolvedUrl = applyPathSubstitutions(url);
504      resolvedUri = appendQuery(resolvedUrl);
505
506      var builder = TransportRequest.builder()
507         .method(method)
508         .uri(resolvedUri)
509         .timeout(timeout);
510
511      // Headers
512      for (var h : headers) {
513         var v = h.getValue();
514         if (v != null)
515            builder.header(h.getName(), v);
516      }
517
518      if (client.parsers != null || client.defaultParser != null) {
519         var hasAccept = headers.stream().anyMatch(h -> "Accept".equalsIgnoreCase(h.getName()));
520         if (! hasAccept) {
521            var accept = client.getDefaultAccept();
522            if (accept != null)
523               builder.header("Accept", accept);
524         }
525      }
526
527      // A Content-Type already present in the header list (a caller-supplied @Header param, or a constant header)
528      // wins over the body's own content type, and is never duplicated by it (dedup invariant).
529      var hasContentType = hasHeader("Content-Type");
530
531      // Pre-converted body from body(Object) takes priority
532      if (convertedBody != null) {
533         if (convertedBody.getContentType() != null && ! hasContentType)
534            builder.header("Content-Type", convertedBody.getContentType());
535         builder.body(convertedBody);
536      } else if (!formData.isEmpty() && body == null) {
537         // Form body when form data is present and no explicit body was set
538         var formBody = buildFormBody();
539         if (! hasContentType)
540            builder.header("Content-Type", formBody.getContentType());
541         builder.body(TransportBody.of(formBody));
542      } else if (body != null) {
543         if (body.getContentType() != null && ! hasContentType)
544            builder.header("Content-Type", body.getContentType());
545         builder.body(TransportBody.of(body));
546      }
547
548      return builder.build();
549   }
550
551   private String applyPathSubstitutions(String template) {
552      var result = template;
553      Object remainder = null;
554      for (var entry : pathData.entrySet()) {
555         if ("/*".equals(entry.getKey())) {
556            remainder = entry.getValue();  // @PathRemainder — applied after named substitutions
557            continue;
558         }
559         var replacement = entry.getValue() != null ? entry.getValue().toString() : "";
560         result = result.replace("{" + entry.getKey() + "}", urlEncode(replacement));
561      }
562      if (remainder != null) {
563         var r = remainder.toString();
564         if (! r.isEmpty()) {
565            if (result.endsWith("/*"))
566               result = result.substring(0, result.length() - 2);
567            if (! result.endsWith("/"))
568               result += "/";
569            result += urlEncodePath(r);
570         }
571      }
572      return result;
573   }
574
575   /** URL-encodes a path remainder while preserving {@code /} segment separators. */
576   private static String urlEncodePath(String value) {
577      var segments = value.split("/", -1);
578      var sb = new StringBuilder();
579      for (var i = 0; i < segments.length; i++) {
580         if (i > 0)
581            sb.append('/');
582         sb.append(urlEncode(segments[i]));
583      }
584      return sb.toString();
585   }
586
587   private URI appendQuery(String baseUrl) {
588      if (queryData.isEmpty()) {
589         try {
590            return new URI(baseUrl);
591         } catch (URISyntaxException e) {
592            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid URL: " + baseUrl, e);
593         }
594      }
595      var sb = new StringBuilder(baseUrl);
596      var first = !baseUrl.contains("?");
597      for (var part : queryData) {
598         var v = part.getValue();
599         if (v == null)
600            continue;
601         sb.append(first ? '?' : '&');
602         sb.append(urlEncode(part.getName())).append('=').append(urlEncode(v));
603         first = false;
604      }
605      try {
606         return new URI(sb.toString());
607      } catch (URISyntaxException e) {
608         throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid URL: " + sb, e);
609      }
610   }
611
612   private HttpBody buildFormBody() {
613      var sb = new StringBuilder();
614      var first = true;
615      for (var part : formData) {
616         var v = part.getValue();
617         if (v == null)
618            continue;
619         if (!first)
620            sb.append('&');
621         sb.append(urlEncode(part.getName())).append('=').append(urlEncode(v));
622         first = false;
623      }
624      final var encoded = sb.toString();
625      return new HttpBody() {
626         @Override public String getContentType() { return "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"; }
627         @Override public long getContentLength() { return encoded.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8).length; }
628         @Override public void writeTo(OutputStream out) throws IOException { out.write(encoded.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8)); }
629         @Override public boolean isRepeatable() { return true; }
630      };
631   }
632
633   private static String urlEncode(String value) {
634      return URLEncoder.encode(value, StandardCharsets.UTF_8);
635   }
636}
